request.prompt:
You are a precise data extraction algorithm. Analyze the following content regarding a possible strike from "ATAC" (the public transport company of Rome, Lazio (12)). IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS: 1. Decide if this document is actually announcing a new/upcoming strike. - If it is a cancellation/revocation of a strike, set isStrike to false. - If it is just providing information about participation (adesione) of a strike that already happened, set isStrike to false. - If it is providing real-time updates about an ongoing strike (e.g., "the metro is currently closed due to a strike"), set isStrike to false. - If it is providing real-time service updates (e.g., metro is open/closed during the strike), set isStrike to false. - ONLY set isStrike to true if it is an announcement of an upcoming strike. 2. If isStrike is true, extract the strike details: - Dates must be in 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' format. - Guaranteed times must be arrays of 'HH:mm-HH:mm' (omitted if not specified). - Location codes should be: 2-digit ISTAT for regions (e.g., "03" for Lombardia), or omitted for national strikes. Example Output if NOT a strike: { "isStrike": false } Example Output if IS a strike: { "isStrike": true, "strikeData": { "startDate": "2024-12-31 08:30:00", "endDate": "2024-12-31 12:30:00", "locationType": "REGIONAL", "locationCodes": ["12"], "guaranteedTimes": ["06:00-09:00", "18:00-21:00"] } } Input Content (Pre-processing: dom-distillation):

Context Metadata

  • Page Title: Sciopero nazionale del trasporto pubblico, ha aderito il 10,1% del personale Atac - Roma Servizi per la Mobilità

Sciopero nazionale del trasporto pubblico, ha aderito il 10,1% del personale Atac

Pubblicato il: 02/05/2023 - Aggiornato il: 02/05/2023

status: 1 L’adesione del personale Atac allo sciopero nazionale del trasporto pubblico di questa mattina è stata pari al 10,1%

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the ATAC Strike Document**

Okay, so the task is pretty straightforward: figure out if this document is announcing a strike or reporting on one that's already happened. The input is a web page discussing the "adhesion percentage" of an ATAC strike—"ha aderito il 10,1% del personale Atac"—that has apparently already occurred. My constraints are clear: I need to produce valid JSON, adhering to a specific schema, no extraneous comments or text.

The metadata itself, "Sciopero nazionale del trasporto pubblico, ha aderito il 10,1% del personale Atac", seems to point towards past participation. Diving into the text itself, the key phrase "L’adesione del personale Atac allo sciopero... di questa mattina è stata pari al 10,1%" clearly indicates that the document is reporting on the participation rate ("adesione") *of this morning's* strike. Therefore, this isn't an announcement, but a post-event report.

Given this analysis, `isStrike` is definitively `false`. Following the instruction to omit `strikeData` if no strike is being announced, I will format the output as `{ "isStrike": false }`. That is the correct JSON to report the outcome.
